param_source_note: 340M parameters per BERT paper (24 layers, 1024 hidden, 16 heads); HF card gives 336M including embeddings — same model, standard citation is 340M. Weights released at google-research/bert under Apache 2.0. Context window = 512 tokens max sequence length (supporting_url HF card confirms).

11B flagship of the T5 family (unified text-to-text encoder-decoder). Released under Apache 2.0 by Google Research. Context window = 512 tokens (standard T5 pretraining sequence length, confirmed in supporting_url HF card and widely in T5 documentation).

8.3B dense GPT-2-style decoder-only transformer. Paper states: 'We open source our code along with the training and evaluation pipelines at github.com/NVIDIA/Megatron-LM'. Training used sequences of 1024 subword units (supporting_url confirms). Briefly the largest dense autoregressive LM (Sep 2019 → Feb 2020). open_weights=false and frontier_open_at_release=false: both cited URLs confirm only training code was open-sourced; neither explicitly states pretrained 8.3B checkpoints were publicly downloadable, so defaulting to false per source-substantiation rule.

17B dense decoder-only transformer (78 layers, hidden size 4256, 28 heads). Trained with sequences of 1024 tokens. Closed-weights: Microsoft released only a private demo to select academic users — weights never publicly downloadable. Largest announced LM in Feb 2020 until superseded by GPT-3 (May 2020).
